observations.recommendation:
1.25 We recommended and Management agreed to direct:

              The City Accountant to:

        a. Reconcile, analyze and investigate further the discrepancy noted
           between the book balances  and  the  RPCPPE  balances  and  make
           adjustments where appropriate;


        b. Provide depreciation for  the  completed  projects   over  their
           useful life by considering the change in the estimated  residual
           value to 5 percent of the cost in accordance with PPSAS from the
           10 per cent under the old NGAS,  for  accurate  presentation  of
           depreciation expense and the  accumulated  depreciation  of  the
           subject PPE in the financial statements;


        c. Analyze further the CIP-Buildings and Other  Structures  account
           and reconcile the posting of payments in the IP which  were  not
           recorded in the CIP account and other PPE accounts and  identify
           the source  document of the reference number as basis in posting
           the payments in the IP;

        d. Review  the CIP account for the non-moving balances  and take up
           the necessary adjustment in the books;

       The City General Services Officer to:


        a. Reconcile, analyze and investigate further the discrepancy noted
           between the book balances  and  the  RPCPPE  balances  and  make
           adjustments where appropriate;


        b. Prepare the RPCPPE by PPE account under the GF and SEF with  the
           Inventory  per  department/office  and  per  school  only  as  a
           reference to the RPCPPE by PPE account in  order  to  facilitate
           the reconciliation of the book and RPCPPE balances.

       The City Engineer to:

        a.  Determine  the  updated  status  of   infrastructure   projects
           completed which remained  recorded  under  the  CIP  account  to
           facilitate the adjustment  of  the  account  and  other  related
           accounts in the accounting records;


        b. Provide the City Accountant with the status of the  above-listed
           uncompleted projects and inform COA of the action undertaken  on
           the  liability  of  the  concerned  contractors  in   case   the
           construction  of  the  said  projects  were  not  completed   in
           accordance with the period of  completion  agreed  upon  in  the
           contracts executed.

2.7   We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City  Accountant
       to reverse the accounting entry made for the  erroneous  entries  of
       the capitalized interests in the books, for accurate presentation of
       the affected accounts in the financial statement.

3.11 We recommended  and  Management  agreed  that  the  City  Accountant
       analyze  and  prepare  journal  entries  for  the  identified   book
       reconciling items reflected in the bank reconciliation and the  same
       be done before closing the books to ensure the accuracy of the  cash
       at the end of each reporting period.


   3.19 We recommended that the  City  Accountant  reconcile  the  different
       balances of the general ledger and subsidiary ledger and ensure that
       the person responsible for recording and posting transactions in the
       book exercise prudent and due care in carrying  out  his/her  duties
       and responsibilities.


   3.30 We recommended and Management agreed to require the City  Accountant
       to reconcile the bank and book balances at all times

   3.35 We recommended and  Management  agreed  that  the  Chief  Accountant
       revert back to the CIB-LCCA and the appropriate  liability  accounts
       checks that remained outstanding for more than six months as of year-
       end.

   3.38 We recommended that  Management  require  the  Chief  Accountant  to
       reverse the  accounting  entries  made  until  all  the  documentary
       requirements are submitted.

   3.43 We recommended that the Management:


        a. Take into consideration the interest rates being offered by each
           Authorized Government Depositary Bank (AGDB); and


        b. Transfer the deposits from PNB and PVB to LBP  which  offer  the
           highest  interest  rate  among  the  four  depository  banks  to
           optimize the earning capacity of cash deposits.

   3.55 We reiterated our recommendation that Management:


       a. Request the PNB to submit  a  copy  of  its  authority  to  accept
          government deposits, if any; and


       b. In absence of the above authority,  the  City  Treasurer  and  the
          concerned city officials adhere and  comply  with  the  Department
          Order  No.  27-05  of  the  DOF  and  the  deposits  with  PNB  be
          transferred immediately to LBP.

4.13 We recommended that Management require the City Treasurer to provide
       justification why the taxes were not collected before the same  were
       prescribed.

   4.21 We recommended that Management require:


       a. The City Treasurer to exert more efforts in the collection of real
          property to ensure prompt payment of the same and utilize all  the
          remedies in the collection of real property taxes available to the
          local government unit prescribed by law; and


       b.  The  City  Accountant  to  reassess  the  reasonableness  of  RPT
          receivables set up at the beginning of every calendar year.

5.    We  recommended that Management require:


       a. The City Treasurer and the BPL Officer to exercise their  mandated
          functions to examine the books of business  establishments  within
          the City’s territorial jurisdiction; and


       b.  The  BPLO  to  conduct   regular   ocular   inspection   of   all
          establishments to identify  those  in  operation  and  appropriate
          action be instituted against  establishments  conducting  business
          without required permits from the City.

   11. We recommended that Management require the City Treasurer to:


       a. Ensure that authority is given only to the authorized personnel to
          amend the declared gross sales/receipts of the taxpayers and  that
          such amendment is evaluated and approved by  higher  official  and
          submission of  any legal basis to support such authority; and


       b. Comply strictly with the policies and procedures of  Republic  Act
          No. 9485 known  as  the  “Anti-Red  Tape  Act  of  2007”  and  the
          Department Heads to continuously monitor their staff.

   5.19 We recommended that Management require the City Treasurer to:


       a. Issue a demand to the contractors identified in the annexes to pay
          their delinquent business permit fees inclusive  of  interest  and
          surcharges, otherwise, issue an order cancelling their license  to
          operate; and


       b. Inspect the books of accounts of other business taxpayers in order
          to determine if the same misrepresentation  is  present  in  other
          taxpayers records aside from the samples taken.

   5.24 We recommended that Management conduct  an  in-depth  study  of  the
       probability to increase to the rates of  business  taxes.  Likewise,
       coordinate with the Sanggunian Panlungsod  for  the  passage  of  an
       ordinance relative to the subject matter.

12. We recommended that Management direct:


       a. The Chief Accountant and the General Services Officer to undertake
          an inventory  reconciliation  process,  the  result  of  the  said
          undertaking should be made the basis  of  Management’s  subsequent
          actions in correcting the inventory accounts;


       b. The Chief Accountant and the General  Services  Officer  to  adopt
          fully the Perpetual Inventory Method by recording all purchases to
          the inventory accounts and record issuances  only  when  supported
          with RISs receipted by the actual end-user;


       c. The General Services Officer to submit the SSMIs on a weekly basis
          and to conduct a physical count of inventories semestrally; and


       d. The Chief Accountant to maintain SLCs.

4.    We recommended that the City Mayor and the Sanggunian  Panglunsod
       consider the lowest interest rates offered by  depository  banks  in
       availing future loans.

5.     We  recommended  and  Management  agreed  to  require  the  City
       Accountant to make the necessary adjustments to correct balances  of
       the AP and the above-stated liability accounts for both the  General
       and Special Education Funds and require  the  concerned  bookkeepers
       assigned to handle the affected  accounts  to  be  apprised  of  the
       proper charging  to  lessen  if  not  totally  eliminate  misleading
       balances in the FS.

16. We recommended that the Local Finance Committee:


       a. Carry out periodic financial performance analysis to  be  able  to
          determine the City’s financial capability, to further improve  its
          collection efficiency and what needs to  be  done  through  proper
          budget allocation,  and  to assess the adequacy  of  the  existing
          internal  control  to  determine  the  underlying  causes  of  the
          weaknesses in the area of cash management;


       b. Closely monitor liquidity condition to see if there is a  need  to
          make adjustments in the CGP’s financial operations by providing  a
          realistic and achievable estimate based on the  trend  of  revenue
          collections; and


       c. Adopt cost cutting measures and strategies  to  improve  its  cash
          position.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the City Accountant to draw journal entry vouchers for the book RIs identified in the BRSs before the closing of the books to ensure the accuracy of the cash account at the end of the reporting period;
Direct the City Treasurer to produce the missing DVs including the supporting documents by demanding the offices concerned and to pinpoint the persons responsible for this infraction so that appropriate administrative action could be meted out otherwise, notice of disallowance will be issued for failure to submit the DVs for post-audit.
For future transactions, instruct all concerned personnel to follow strictly the procedures in the handling and custody of financial documents.
Demand from the Managers of depository banks the timely submission of BS.
observations.reason:
JEVs for the RIs were drawn and effected in the books for some of the identified RIs.
Dep Ed Pasay City thru Mr. Arellano produced photocopies of the DV but still to submit the certifications from the payees that payments were already received to include certified copies of ORs issued by the payees.
CTO will no longer allow anybody to borrow the paid DVs. A memorandum has been issued to the concerned accountable officer to this effect.
If the official BS is not available, the OCA consistently requests for computer print-outs of the monthly transactions.
Despite follow-ups, the concerned banks failed to submit the BS within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
